cases.verdict: Personal independent sites remain the strongest website line. Structure, tone, anchor navigation, about section, visual identity, and portfolio-card density are materially better than the earlier generic landing output, and the latest baseline now also pushes the page further toward an authored personal-brand surface through stronger default voice plus clearer signature framing. The hero entrance now communicates a clearer personal persona plus a more explicit author-signature layer, the default hero claim sounds more like a point of view than a category label, the about/collaboration/contact language reads more like a real working method with one coherent narrator behind it, and the portfolio area now also reads more like a curated case shelf: the three portfolio cards separate more clearly into different case types, the cards themselves read more like compact project cards because they expose project type, delivery scope, and result, and the latest signature pass now also adds a lighter editorial header note, ordered case-sequence markers with short editor notes, and a footer signoff strip that make the page feel more intentionally authored from entrance to ending. The line is now backed by refreshed full-page runtime proof for this stronger signature-led baseline, while the contact-submit success behavior remains previously runtime-verified.
confirmed_signals: hero persona chips that clarify how the person works
about copy now reads more like an independent-consultant working method
collaboration feedback intro now describes a clearer working rhythm
contact and testimonial copy now read more like one authored voice
more specific case-detail lines in portfolio cards
clearer differentiation between consultant-homepage, product-intro, and editorial/content case types
project-card style portfolio structure with explicit type/scope/result fields
previously verified real contact-submit success state
limits: visual system is stronger but can still be more distinctive
brand personality is stronger now but still not yet a highly distinctive authored personal-brand system
case stories are more concrete now but can still become more individually distinctive and outcome-rich
personal voice is more coherent now but can still become even more memorable and less safe

cases.verdict: Company and product websites now have stronger product-positioning language, a more complete product-story structure, a more believable trust/FAQ layer, a clearer product-brand treatment, stronger sentence-level homepage copy in the rendered page, a clearer first-screen product-system information layer, a tighter hero line that behaves more like a homepage claim, and a stronger final contact/demo capture surface plus a clearer intake-oriented contact form. Generated project names no longer leak into the hero, feature bullets help explain fit and flow, customer proof is represented as quote / role / result cards, and the rendered page now gives both trust and brand surfaces more visible structure. The hero entrance carries a clearer brand-row layer plus a compact brand-grid layer, and the first screen now has stronger rhythm and homepage tension through larger headline scale, roomier spacing, more assertive CTA sizing, more direct scannable fit/scenario/action metadata, a shorter, cleaner headline, a clearer linked hero-copy block that visually binds the headline/subtitle area to the sequenced brand-grid cards, a more coherent decision-band grouping for the rule-strip plus CTA actions, a slightly clearer title lockup that gives the headline a more explicit visual anchor without adding new copy, a clearer primary CTA emphasis that makes the first action read more like the obvious next move in the page, and a calmer secondary CTA treatment that makes the browse path feel more exploratory and less competitive. In the generic fallback case, the hero no longer depends on a clumsy `品牌官网｜...` title prefix and instead reads more like a standalone homepage claim. The latest first-screen wording is also cleaner, because the hero claim now lands as `先把产品价值讲明白`, the secondary CTA reads more like an immediate browsing choice, the brand-row tag now reads more like a short memory cue than a generic system label, the three first-screen brand cards now read more like a sequenced entrance instead of visually similar info cards, the headline/subtitle block now reads less like a detached text area and more like part of the same first-screen brand entrance, the rule-strip plus CTA now read more like one compact decision block, the headline itself now carries a cleaner underline-style emphasis instead of relying only on scale, the primary CTA now carries a more obvious forward cue and stronger visual weight than the secondary browse action, and the secondary action now carries a quieter browse-arrow cue with a lighter visual treatment. The trust cards now also carry clearer scenario context, so they read more like real team retrospectives instead of abstract praise blocks. The FAQ layer now also reads more like pre-sales guidance, because default items now carry scenario context and short follow-up notes instead of only generic placeholder answers, the rendered FAQ surface is easier to scan before opening individual entries, and expanded answers now separate core judgment from the next recommended step. The contact/CTA ending layer now also reads more like a branded product-site handoff: it frames the close as demo/contact capture, exposes response timing and discussion modes, adds a clearer handoff-card layer before the form, and gives the last screen a more scannable consultation-oriented next step. The contact form itself now also reads more like a real consultation intake surface, because it tells visitors what to send before the form starts, labels fields more explicitly, uses more realistic enterprise placeholders, and sets expectations for the first reply. The rendered contact section now also has a stronger conversion-oriented hierarchy, because it separates the prep guidance from the actual form panel, gives the right-hand form a clearer card surface, and adds a more explicit submit-hint line under the main action. The rendered contact section now also has a stronger pre-submit conversion feel, because the message field now explains what kind of detail is enough to get started, the main action has a clearer submitted state, and the generated form now includes a built-in success card with follow-up guidance for the post-submit state. The first screen now also carries a slightly stronger memory layer, because a compact three-step strip reinforces the explanation order before the visitor chooses a CTA. The rendered page now also carries a clearer below-the-fold section rhythm, because the product-introduction, feature, trust, FAQ, and contact sections now expose compact current/next path cues that make the page read more like one sequenced narrative path instead of isolated blocks. The ending layer now also reads more like a real closing loop, because the CTA surface no longer stops at a single button row and instead exposes a short three-step expectation strip that tells visitors to send the current stage, wait for a homepage recommendation, and then decide how to proceed. The FAQ-to-contact transition is now also clearer in the generated page, because the FAQ section now closes with an explicit bridge into contact and the contact section begins with a matching short intake-oriented entry cue. The contact section now also opens with a clearer decision-to-intake bridge, so visitors are explicitly told to carry stage, goal, and desired handoff action into the consultation form instead of dropping straight from judgment mode into raw fields. The post-submit state now also reads more like part of the same closing loop, because the CTA surface can now reflect a short success-to-next-step bridge instead of leaving the submitted state visually isolated from the final CTA path. The final page landing is now also clearer, because the footer can now expose a short final landing bridge after submission instead of dropping straight from CTA closure into a generic footer. The lower closing path is now also tighter between intake and CTA, because the CTA section now opens with a dedicated intake-to-closure bridge instead of dropping straight from the contact form into generic capture copy. The mid-page sequence is now also tighter, because the product-introduction and feature sections no longer hand off as plain adjacent blocks: the rendered page now inserts short proof-bridge surfaces between introduction, feature, and trust, so the middle of the homepage reads more like one proof path instead of three isolated sections. The proof/decsion path is now also tighter between trust and FAQ, because the testimonials section now hands off through a dedicated trust-to-FAQ bridge instead of dropping straight into the FAQ block. The submitted-state closing path is now also tighter between CTA closure and the final footer landing, because the CTA section now exposes a dedicated closure-to-landing bridge instead of jumping straight from success language into the footer. The latest runtime pass also confirmed that the lower closing path now holds together after a real contact submission, because POST SUBMIT, CLOSURE TO LANDING, and FINAL LANDING all render in sequence instead of staying only as generated structural cues. The rendered page now also carries a clearer stage-by-stage brand rhythm through more differentiated section tones and proof-bridge accents, so the middle and lower sections feel less like one repeated blue system and more like a sequenced product-site path. The rendered page now also carries a clearer brand-posture layer, so the company baseline feels less like a safe branded shell and more like a team with a recognisable point of view: the header now opens with a short brand-note strip, the hero now exposes a dedicated BRAND POSTURE statement plus posture cards, and the footer now closes with a FINAL BRAND LINE signoff strip while keeping the stronger proof / intake / closure path intact.

cases.verdict: The storefront line is real and now cleaner. The route loop remains valid for home, product detail, cart, and checkout, while the latest runtime review confirmed a more coherent storefront shell, no broken after-sales entry from the ecom homepage, no default dependency on empty /api/products or /api/order/submit proxies when no backend is present, a stronger confirm-and-complete checkout surface, a denser cart surface that reads more like a real pre-checkout page, a clearer bridge between cart and checkout as parts of the same purchase flow, a more believable post-submit completion state that remains inside the storefront, a stronger in-page add-to-cart feedback surface on product detail, a more stable product-detail purchase surface with system-owned mock imagery, a clearer product-decision layer inside the product surface, a fuller product-media side and a more believable related-picks surface on product detail, a clearer visual continuity layer between homepage product cards and product detail, a stronger homepage reading rhythm across hero, product sections, and shop continuation, a clearer homepage filter surface between search, category narrowing, visible product counts, and product sections, cleaner search/category browsing surfaces that keep the same storefront filter language, a stronger low-result discovery layout for search/category pages, a clearer discovery-to-shop snapshot layer on search/category surfaces, a clearer shop-continuity surface that now keeps the shop page inside the same browsing system instead of leaving it as a disconnected store stub, a stronger shop-to-product continuity layer that now keeps product detail inside the same in-store browsing path instead of discarding the shop context, plus a clearer visual bridge between the shop hero flow and the product-detail purchase surface, a runtime-confirmed discovery bridge that now preserves whether the shop page was entered from category browsing or from search-driven narrowing, a stronger search/category-to-shop-to-product continuity layer that now keeps discovery context visible inside product detail, a stronger search/category-to-shop-to-product-to-cart continuity layer that now keeps that same discovery context visible once users enter the cart, a stronger cart-to-checkout continuity layer that now keeps both purchase handoff and discovery-memory language visible once users move into checkout, plus a stronger checkout-to-completion return layer that now keeps source-aware browse paths visible after a real submit flow. The cart-and-checkout spine is now also tighter, because both surfaces expose a dedicated purchase-axis bridge that keeps discovery memory, product handoff, and final confirmation inside the same decision path instead of treating them as adjacent but separate blocks. The completion-state return path is now also tighter, because checkout now exposes a dedicated RETURN AXIS bridge that makes purchase closure, retained order summary, and continue-browsing routes read more like the same post-submit path instead of adjacent retention blocks. The front half is now also tighter, because search and category surfaces expose a dedicated shop-snapshot board before the product grid, and direct entry into product detail now preserves discovery context even without routing through the shop page first. The latest runtime check also confirmed that direct product URLs with discovery=search and discovery=category preserve the same discovery memory without requiring an intermediate shop hop.

no claim should be made for merchant backend, complex payments, or operations tooling

cases.verdict: The after-sales line is now stronger as a service-center surface with early handling-flow structure and a clearer in-progress case-ops summary. It still centers on a dedicated after-sales route, but the rendered page now carries clearer order context, service-routing actions, contact-channel framing, a visible service timeline, a current-case-status layer, a pre-submit preparation checklist, and a more explicit latest-update/next-step/current-owner/materials summary instead of only a minimal support-entry layout. The generated after-sales page now also carries a clearer case-history/recent-updates layer, so it reads more like an in-progress handling surface than only a service-center page. The latest generated surface now also carries a clearer case-flow track that makes the current handling step, current checkpoint, and next move easier to scan at a glance. The latest runtime-checked surface now also carries a clearer bridge between case flow, current ops, and recent history, and the footer now stays inside the after-sales route instead of exposing broken exits. The latest runtime-checked surface now also uses explicit bridge cards between case flow, current ops, and recent history, so the middle of the page reads more like one connected handling track instead of three adjacent boards. The lower half of the runtime-checked surface now also uses explicit bridge cards from recent history into action selection and then into the active panel, so the page reads more like one continuous handling path instead of separate records, actions, and instructions. The runtime-checked ending layer now also uses a final bridge from the active panel into the footer actions, so the page closes more like one continuous handling path instead of dropping from instructions into generic navigation. The latest click-tested runtime surface now also places the active panel before the timeline and prep boards, with an explicit support bridge after the panel, so the lower half reads more like choose action, review instructions, then check timing and materials. The latest click-tested runtime surface now also gives the chosen action card a clearer selected state, current-choice badge, and view-current-instructions treatment, so the active path reads more clearly before users continue into the instruction panel. The latest runtime-backed default entry surface now also gives the no-order state a clearer intake layer, with an explicit case-intake checklist and a prepare-intake-to-choose-action bridge, so the route reads more like a real service intake page before any order context is carried in. The latest runtime-backed pair of screenshots now also separates the default no-order intake route from the order-carried handling route more clearly, with distinct entry-mode surfaces that make it easier to tell whether the page is still preparing intake or has already entered a tracked case path. The latest tracked-case runtime surface now also carries a clearer case snapshot with the current node, current owner, handling SLA, and next move, so the order-carried route reads more like a real in-progress case board instead of only a generic order-context box. The latest runtime-backed tracked-case review now also inserts a clearer CASE BOARD bridge between CASE SNAPSHOT, CASE STATUS, and CASE FLOW, so the top half reads more like one continuous case board instead of one snapshot box followed by two separate boards. The latest runtime-backed tracked-case review now also adds a clearer board-summary strip that aligns owner, current focus, and next move before the status and flow boards, so the route reads more like one active case board instead of a snapshot box followed by separate downstream sections. The latest runtime-backed tracked-case review now also adds a clearer STATUS TO FLOW bridge and a stronger status-aware handoff into CASE OPS, so the middle of the page reads more like one continuous handling axis from status to flow to current ops. The generated tracked-case route now also keeps owner, SLA, and current focus visible in a short continuity strip between CASE FLOW and CASE OPS, so that handoff reads less like a drop between two boards and more like one continuous handling axis. The generated tracked-case route now also keeps latest update, owner, and next record hint visible in a short continuity strip before CASE HISTORY, so the transition out of CASE OPS reads more like one continuous handling path instead of a jump into the record list. The generated tracked-case route now also keeps the current record anchor, current suggestion, and next step visible in a short action-entry strip before the action cards, so the transition out of CASE HISTORY reads more like one continuous handling path instead of a jump into raw action choice. The generated tracked-case route now also keeps the chosen action, current suggestion, and next step visible in a short continuity strip before the active panel, so the transition out of action selection reads more like one continuous handling path instead of a jump into raw instructions. The generated tracked-case route now also keeps the current instruction, current SLA, and next step visible in a short continuity strip before the support bridge, so the transition out of the active panel reads more like one continuous handling path into timeline and prep guidance instead of a jump into downstream boards. The generated tracked-case route now also keeps current preparation, current owner, and next move visible in a short continuity strip before the final footer actions, so the ending reads more like one continuous handling path instead of a drop from support boards into raw navigation. The latest runtime pass also confirmed both entry modes again in delivery-state output, and a real tracked-case click flow now shows the selected action state, active panel, support bridge, and footer closure together instead of leaving the lower-half handling path only generator-backed.

product_truth: Personal Independent Site is currently the strongest quality line; it now reads much more like a real portfolio and personal-brand site, with stronger visual identity, clearer persona, more realistic project-card structure, and a more connected story between About, work examples, and collaboration rhythm.

Company / Product Website is now much closer to a branded product-site system: the first screen is stronger, trust and FAQ layers are more realistic, the contact/demo capture surface is more credible, the final CTA/contact handoff now carries a clearer homepage-closing loop, the lower closing path now also carries clearer intake-to-closure and closure-to-landing bridges, and the page reads more like a coherent product homepage rather than a generic landing template, and the middle of the page now carries clearer proof bridges between introduction, features, and trust.

Ecommerce Independent Storefront is a real minimal storefront loop with a cleaner shell, cleaner no-backend defaults, a stronger product add-feedback surface, a denser cart surface, a clearer cart-to-checkout flow bridge, a more believable post-submit completion state, a more stable product-detail purchase surface with system-owned mock imagery, clearer home-to-detail visual continuity, a stronger homepage reading rhythm, a clearer homepage filter surface, cleaner search/category browsing surfaces, a stronger shop-continuity surface, a clearer shop-to-product continuity layer, a clearer shop-to-product visual bridge, a stronger search/category-to-shop-to-product continuity layer, stronger search/category-to-shop-to-product-to-cart continuity, clearer product-to-cart decision continuity, stronger cart-to-checkout continuity, clearer cart/checkout purchase-axis bridges, and runtime-confirmed checkout-to-completion return layers that now read like the same storefront discovery-and-purchase system, and a clearer completion return-axis bridge, plus a clearer discovery-to-shop snapshot layer on search/category surfaces and direct discovery context retention on direct product entry.

After-Sales Service Website now reads more like a service-center surface with early handling-flow, case-ops structure, a clearer case-history layer, a clearer case-flow track, a clearer flow-to-ops-to-history bridge, a clearer snapshot-to-status-to-flow axis, a clearer board-summary strip before status/flow, a clearer status-to-flow-to-ops handling axis, a clearer flow-to-ops continuity strip, a clearer ops-to-history continuity strip, and a clearer ops-to-history-to-actions handling axis plus a clearer history-to-actions continuity strip before concrete action choice, a clearer actions-to-panel continuity strip before active instructions, a clearer panel-to-support continuity strip before timeline/prep guidance, and a clearer support-to-footer continuity strip before final actions in the tracked-case route, rather than only a support-entry page.
Across all four supported website packs, the system is now beyond purely structural generation; it is generating more convincing website surfaces with clearer product, service, and conversion layers.
The supported website frontier is still website-first. None of these lines should yet be positioned as full application platforms, merchant backends, CMS systems, or operations software.
